parts.title: SKILL I: FOCUS ON THE LAST LINE

questions.passage:
(woman) How soon will you be leaving?
(man)   I'm on my way now.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He is leaving now.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the man's response 'I'm on my way now' directly answers the woman's question about when he is leaving. This indicates he is departing at the present moment.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, respons pria 'I'm on my way now' secara langsung menjawab pertanyaan wanita tentang kapan dia akan pergi. Ini menunjukkan bahwa dia berangkat pada saat ini.

options.text: He has to go' out of his way.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The man's statement 'I'm on my way now' does not imply that he has to go out of his way; it simply means he is currently en route.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Pernyataan pria 'I'm on my way now' tidak menyiratkan bahwa dia harus pergi keluar dari jalurnya; itu hanya berarti dia sedang dalam perjalanan.

options.text: He will not be leaving soon.

reason.en: This option is the opposite of what the man means. His response 'I'm on my way now' clearly indicates he is leaving soon, not that he will not be leaving soon.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang dimaksud pria itu. Responsnya 'I'm on my way now' dengan jelas menunjukkan bahwa dia akan segera pergi, bukan bahwa dia tidak akan segera pergi.

options.text: He will do it his own way.

reason.en: This option is irrelevant to the conversation. The man's statement is about his departure time, not about doing something in his own way.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak relevan dengan percakapan. Pernyataan pria itu adalah tentang waktu keberangkatannya, bukan tentang melakukan sesuatu dengan caranya sendiri.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 1 - Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man)   Was Steve able to get into the house?
(woman) I left the door unlocked for him.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN ASSUME STEVE DID?

options.text: He locked the door.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman states she left the door unlocked, implying Steve could enter without needing to lock it.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita itu menyatakan dia membiarkan pintu tidak terkunci, menyiratkan Steve bisa masuk tanpa perlu menguncinya.

options.text: He tried unsuccessfully to get into the house.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman's statement 'I left the door unlocked for him' suggests she expected him to be able to get in, not that he failed.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan wanita 'Saya membiarkan pintu tidak terkunci untuknya' menyiratkan dia mengharapkan Steve bisa masuk, bukan bahwa dia gagal.

options.text: He was able to open the door.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the woman's last line, 'I left the door unlocked for him,' implies that she assumes Steve was able to get into the house because the door was unlocked.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, baris terakhir wanita, 'Saya membiarkan pintu tidak terkunci untuknya,' menyiratkan bahwa dia berasumsi Steve dapat masuk ke rumah karena pintu tidak terkunci.

options.text: He left the house without locking the door.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue is about Steve getting into the house, not about him leaving it. The woman's action was to facilitate his entry.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog ini tentang Steve masuk ke rumah, bukan tentang dia meninggalkannya. Tindakan wanita itu adalah untuk memfasilitasi masuknya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 1 - Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man)   The dinner special is roast turkey with mashed potatoes and gravy, and apple pie for dessert.
(woman) That doesn't sound good to me.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She doesn't like to listen to turkeys.

reason.en: This answer is a distractor based on similar sounds. The woman is not talking about listening to turkeys, but about the dinner special which includes roast turkey.
reason.id: Jawaban ini adalah pengecoh berdasarkan bunyi yang mirip. Wanita itu tidak berbicara tentang mendengarkan kalkun, tetapi tentang menu spesial makan malam yang mencakup kalkun panggang.

options.text: She thinks the dinner sounds special.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because the woman explicitly states 'That doesn't sound good to me,' which is the opposite of thinking the dinner sounds special.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Itu tidak terdengar enak bagiku,' yang berlawanan dengan menganggap makan malam itu spesial.

options.text: She especially likes the roast turkey.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect. The woman's statement 'That doesn't sound good to me' directly contradicts the idea that she especially likes the roast turkey. The answer uses 'especially' which is a synonym for 'special' in the context of the dinner special, creating a potential trap.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah. Pernyataan wanita 'Itu tidak terdengar enak bagiku' secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia sangat menyukai kalkun panggang. Jawaban ini menggunakan 'especially' yang merupakan sinonim dari 'special' dalam konteks menu spesial makan malam, menciptakan jebakan potensial.

options.text: She'd prefer a different dinner.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman's statement 'That doesn't sound good to me' implies she would prefer something else. This is a paraphrase of her negative reaction to the dinner special, meaning she'd prefer a different dinner.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan wanita 'Itu tidak terdengar enak bagiku' menyiratkan bahwa dia lebih memilih sesuatu yang lain. Ini adalah parafrase dari reaksinya yang negatif terhadap menu spesial makan malam, yang berarti dia lebih memilih makan malam yang berbeda.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman)Could you help me with my physics homework tonight? I'm really havingtrouble with it.
(man)  Sorry, I'm busy tonight.
questions.question: 4.WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY?

options.text: He'll be busy with her homework tonight.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the man states he is busy tonight, implying he cannot help *tonight*, not that he will be busy with *her* homework.
reason.id: Ini salah karena pria itu menyatakan dia sibuk malam ini, menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak bisa membantu *malam ini*, bukan bahwa dia akan sibuk dengan pekerjaan rumah *wanita itu*.

options.text: He can't help her tonight.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the man's statement 'Sorry, I'm busy tonight' is a polite way of saying he cannot help her at that specific time. The word 'Sorry' indicates a negative response to the request.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pernyataan pria 'Sorry, I'm busy tonight' adalah cara sopan untuk mengatakan bahwa dia tidak bisa membantunya pada waktu tertentu. Kata 'Sorry' menunjukkan respons negatif terhadap permintaan tersebut.

options.text: He's sorry he can't ever help her.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man only states he is busy *tonight*. He does not say he can *never* help her. This is an overgeneralization.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pria itu hanya menyatakan dia sibuk *malam ini*. Dia tidak mengatakan bahwa dia *tidak pernah* bisa membantunya. Ini adalah generalisasi yang berlebihan.

options.text: He'll help her with her physics.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man explicitly states he is busy and therefore cannot help. He does not offer to help her with her physics.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia sibuk dan oleh karena itu tidak dapat membantu. Dia tidak menawarkan untuk membantunya dengan fisika.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions)

questions.passage:
(man)   What did you think of Professor Martin's lecture on the migratory habits of whales?
(woman) I couldn't keep my eyes open.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Her eyes hurt.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'I couldn't keep my eyes open' is an idiom that means she was very tired or bored, not that her eyes physically hurt.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'I couldn't keep my eyes open' adalah sebuah idiom yang berarti dia sangat lelah atau bosan, bukan bahwa matanya sakit secara fisik.

options.text: She thought the lecture was great.

reason.en: The woman's statement is a negative expression implying the opposite of what is stated. If she couldn't keep her eyes open, she did not think the lecture was great.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita adalah ekspresi negatif yang menyiratkan kebalikan dari apa yang dinyatakan. Jika dia tidak bisa menjaga matanya tetap terbuka, dia tidak menganggap kuliah itu hebat.

options.text: The class was boring.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 13: Listen for Emphatic Expressions of Surprise, the idiom 'I couldn't keep my eyes open' is an emphatic way of saying that the lecture was very boring, causing her to feel sleepy.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 13: Listen for Emphatic Expressions of Surprise, idiom 'I couldn't keep my eyes open' adalah cara yang menekankan untuk mengatakan bahwa kuliah itu sangat membosankan, membuatnya merasa mengantuk.

options.text: She didn't want to watch Professor Martin.

reason.en: The woman's comment is about the lecture itself, not about Professor Martin personally. There is no indication she wanted to avoid watching him.
reason.id: Komentar wanita adalah tentang kuliah itu sendiri, bukan tentang Profesor Martin secara pribadi. Tidak ada indikasi bahwa dia ingin menghindari menontonnya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skill 13: Listen for Emphatic Expressions of Surprise)

questions.passage:
(woman)Have this month's bills been paid, or is that something we need to take care of now?
(man)  I paid the phone and electricity, but not the credit cards.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Not all the bills have been paid.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the man's statement 'I paid the phone and electricity, but not the credit cards' directly implies that not all bills have been paid, making this the correct inference.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pernyataan pria 'I paid the phone and electricity, but not the credit cards' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa tidak semua tagihan telah dibayar, menjadikannya kesimpulan yang benar.

options.text: They don't have enough credit to pay the bills.

reason.en: This option introduces information about credit that is not mentioned in the dialogue. The dialogue focuses on whether bills have been paid, not on the amount of credit available.
reason.id: Opsi ini memperkenalkan informasi tentang kredit yang tidak disebutkan dalam dialog. Dialog berfokus pada apakah tagihan telah dibayar, bukan pada jumlah kredit yang tersedia.

options.text: What she said on the phone was not credible.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the dialogue by bringing in the concept of 'phone calls' and 'credibility,' which are not discussed. The dialogue is about paying bills.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan dialog dengan membawa konsep 'panggilan telepon' dan 'kredibilitas', yang tidak dibahas. Dialog ini tentang membayar tagihan.

options.text: He used a credit card to pay some of the bills.

reason.en: While the man did pay some bills, this option incorrectly states that he used a credit card for 'some of the bills.' The dialogue specifies he paid the phone and electricity bills, and implies the credit card bills are *not* yet paid.
reason.id: Meskipun pria itu membayar beberapa tagihan, opsi ini secara keliru menyatakan bahwa dia menggunakan kartu kredit untuk 'beberapa tagihan'. Dialog tersebut merinci bahwa dia membayar tagihan telepon dan listrik, dan menyiratkan bahwa tagihan kartu kredit *belum* dibayar.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 7 - Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(man)  Will you be able to get back from running all your errands by four o'clock?
(woman) I'll be back as quickly as I can.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY THAT SHE'LL DO?

options.text: She'll call back quickly.

reason.en: The woman says she will return quickly, not that she will call back.
reason.id: Wanita itu mengatakan dia akan kembali dengan cepat, bukan menelepon kembali.

options.text: She'll definitely be back by 4:00.

reason.en: The woman says she will return 'as quickly as I can,' which is not a guarantee of being back by 4:00. It implies she will try her best but cannot promise it.
reason.id: Wanita itu mengatakan dia akan kembali 'sesegera mungkin', yang bukan jaminan akan kembali pada jam 4:00. Ini menyiratkan dia akan berusaha sebaik mungkin tetapi tidak bisa menjanjikannya.

options.text: She'll give it back by 4:00.

reason.en: The woman is talking about returning from errands, not giving something back.
reason.id: Wanita itu berbicara tentang kembali dari urusan, bukan mengembalikan sesuatu.

options.text: She'll try to return fast.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the phrase 'as quickly as I can' is a paraphrase for 'try to return fast'. The word 'quickly' is a synonym for 'fast', and 'as I can' implies an attempt or effort.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, frasa 'as quickly as I can' adalah parafrasa untuk 'try to return fast'. Kata 'quickly' adalah sinonim dari 'fast', dan 'as I can' menyiratkan sebuah upaya atau usaha.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man)   Have you seen Tim? I really need to talk with him about the phone bill.
(woman) Well,... he was here just a minute ago.
questions.question: 8.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT TIM?

options.text: She hasn't seen Tim.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman implies she saw Tim, stating 'he was here just a minute ago.'
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita itu menyiratkan bahwa dia melihat Tim, menyatakan 'dia ada di sini beberapa saat yang lalu.'

options.text: Tim was there only for a moment.

reason.en: This is incorrect. While Tim was there 'just a minute ago,' the phrase 'only for a moment' is not directly supported and might imply a very brief presence, which isn't the primary meaning.
reason.id: Ini salah. Meskipun Tim ada di sana 'beberapa saat yang lalu,' frasa 'hanya sesaat' tidak didukung secara langsung dan mungkin menyiratkan kehadiran yang sangat singkat, yang bukan merupakan makna utama.

options.text: Tim was around a short time ago.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman's statement 'he was here just a minute ago' is a paraphrase of Tim being around a short time ago. 'Just a minute ago' is synonymous with 'a short time ago'.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan wanita 'dia ada di sini beberapa saat yang lalu' adalah parafrase dari Tim berada di sekitar waktu yang singkat yang lalu. 'Beberapa saat yang lalu' identik dengan 'waktu yang singkat yang lalu'.

options.text: Tim will return in a minute.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue provides no information about when Tim will return; it only states when he was last seen.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog tidak memberikan informasi tentang kapan Tim akan kembali; itu hanya menyatakan kapan dia terakhir terlihat.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man)  There's a car parked in my spot even though the sign says that this space is reserved.
(woman) I guess we'll have to park somewhere else.
questions.question: 9.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She doesn't like the place he chose.

reason.en: This is an incorrect inference. The woman's statement is a direct consequence of the parking situation, not a personal preference about the location itself.
reason.id: Ini adalah kesimpulan yang salah. Pernyataan wanita itu adalah konsekuensi langsung dari situasi parkir, bukan preferensi pribadi tentang lokasi itu sendiri.

options.text: She doesn't want to get into the car.

reason.en: This is irrelevant to the dialogue. The conversation is about parking a car, not about getting into it.
reason.id: Ini tidak relevan dengan dialog. Percakapan ini tentang memarkir mobil, bukan tentang masuk ke dalamnya.

options.text: She's glad the spot is reserved.

reason.en: This is the opposite of what the woman implies. The sign stating the space is reserved is the reason they cannot park there, so she is not glad about it.
reason.id: Ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang tersirat oleh wanita itu. Tanda yang menyatakan bahwa tempat itu disediakan adalah alasan mengapa mereka tidak bisa parkir di sana, jadi dia tidak senang tentang itu.

options.text: They can't park the car there.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the woman's statement 'I guess we'll have to park somewhere else' is a direct implication of the man's statement that the car is parked in his reserved spot. The reserved sign means they cannot park there, leading to the conclusion that they must find another place.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pernyataan wanita 'I guess we'll have to park somewhere else' adalah implikasi langsung dari pernyataan pria bahwa mobil diparkir di tempat yang disediakan untuknya. Tanda yang menyatakan tempat itu disediakan berarti mereka tidak bisa parkir di sana, yang mengarah pada kesimpulan bahwa mereka harus mencari tempat lain.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman)Do we have enough food for all the guests who are attending the reception this evening?
(man)  The refrigerator's about to burst.
questions.question: 10.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: There's plenty to eat.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'The refrigerator's about to burst' is an idiom meaning it is very full. This implies there is plenty of food, which is a synonym for 'plenty to eat'.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan pria 'The refrigerator's about to burst' adalah sebuah idiom yang berarti kulkas sangat penuh. Ini menyiratkan bahwa ada banyak makanan, yang merupakan sinonim dari 'plenty to eat'.

options.text: The refrigerator's broken.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The idiom 'about to burst' refers to being full, not broken. The man is not indicating a malfunction.
reason.id: Ini salah. Idiom 'about to burst' mengacu pada keadaan penuh, bukan rusak. Pria itu tidak menunjukkan adanya kerusakan.

options.text: The food isn't in the refrigerator.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The idiom 'about to burst' implies the food is inside the refrigerator, making it full. The statement does not suggest the food is elsewhere.
reason.id: Ini salah. Idiom 'about to burst' menyiratkan bahwa makanan ada di dalam kulkas, membuatnya penuh. Pernyataan tersebut tidak menyarankan bahwa makanan berada di tempat lain.

options.text: He's not sure if there's enough.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The idiom 'about to burst' strongly suggests the refrigerator is full, indicating certainty about having enough food, not uncertainty.
reason.id: Ini salah. Idiom 'about to burst' sangat menyarankan bahwa kulkas penuh, menunjukkan kepastian tentang kecukupan makanan, bukan ketidakpastian.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) What did you think of the final exam in algebra?
(man) It was too easy!
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The final exam was harder than the others.

reason.en: The man said the exam was 'too easy,' which is the opposite of 'harder than the others.' This is a direct contradiction.
reason.id: Pria itu mengatakan ujiannya 'terlalu mudah', yang merupakan kebalikan dari 'lebih sulit dari yang lain'. Ini adalah kontradiksi langsung.

options.text: There were two exams rather than one.

reason.en: The dialogue mentions only one exam ('the final exam'). There is no information about there being two exams.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut hanya menyebutkan satu ujian ('ujian akhir'). Tidak ada informasi tentang adanya dua ujian.

options.text: He thought the exam would be easier.

reason.en: The man's statement 'It was too easy!' implies he found it easy, not that he expected it to be easier. If he expected it to be easier, he would likely be disappointed, but his statement is positive.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'Itu terlalu mudah!' menyiratkan bahwa dia menganggapnya mudah, bukan bahwa dia mengharapkannya lebih mudah. Jika dia mengharapkannya lebih mudah, dia mungkin akan kecewa, tetapi pernyataannya positif.

options.text: The exam was not very difficult.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'It was too easy!' means that the exam was not difficult. Option 3 paraphrases this meaning using synonyms.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan pria itu 'Itu terlalu mudah!' berarti ujian itu tidak sulit. Opsi 3 memparafrasekan makna ini menggunakan sinonim.
